sizing_rule.note: Dynamic fractional f with active equity percentage (FRAC): Position Size = Account Equity × FRAC × f. Dynamic approach overtakes static allocation when initial active equity allocation < 100% (1.0) and geometric mean G > 1.0. FRAC is the active equity percentage applied to account equity; optimal f derived from portfolio analysis.

source_rule_text: Use dynamic fractional f with an active equity percentage (FRAC) to determine position sizing, where the dynamic approach will eventually overtake static allocation strategies, particularly when geometric mean is greater than 1.0.
